perturbation.original_text: (c) The Servicer shall have full power and authority, acting alone or through any party properly designated by it hereunder, including any Sub-Servicer, to do any and all things in connection with such servicing and administration which it may deem necessary or desirable, consistent with the terms of this Servicing Agreement and the Servicing Standard. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, unless such power is revoked by the Owner on account of the occurrence of a Servicer Default, the Servicer shall have full power and authority (i) to make withdrawals from the applicable servicer collection account permitted by the terms of this Servicing Agreement, the Loan Agreement or any other Loan Document and (ii) to execute and deliver, on behalf of the Owner, any and all instruments of satisfaction or cancellation, or of partial or full release or discharge, and all other comparable instruments, with respect to the Receivables and, after the delinquency of any Receivable and to the extent permitted under and in compliance with applicable Servicing Standard and the Loan Agreement, to commence collection proceedings with respect to such Receivables.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) The Servicer shall have limited power and authority, acting alone or through any party properly designated by it hereunder, including any Sub-Servicer, to do only specifically enumerated actions in connection with such servicing and administration which it may deem necessary or desirable, consistent with the terms of this Servicing Agreement and the Servicing Standard. Unless such power is revoked by the Owner on account of the occurrence of a Servicer Default, the Servicer shall have power and authority only to (i) make withdrawals from the applicable servicer collection account permitted by the terms of this Servicing Agreement, the Loan Agreement or any other Loan Document and (ii) to execute and deliver, on behalf of the Owner, any and all instruments of satisfaction or cancellation, or of partial or full release or discharge, and all other comparable instruments, with respect to the Receivables.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'full power and authority' to 'limited power and authority' and explicitly limiting the Servicer's actions to only (i) and (ii), we remove the general grant of authority to 'commence collection proceedings with respect to such Receivables' after delinquency. This omission contradicts the Servicing Standard defined in Section 1(b) which requires servicing and administering each related Serviced Asset. Without the authority to commence collection proceedings, the Servicer cannot fully service delinquent receivables, creating a contradiction within Section 1(c) itself. The text further states that Servicer actions should be consistent with 'Servicing Standard'. But without the power to collect, the Servicer can't achieve it, creating an in-text contradiction in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) The Servicer shall have limited power and authority, acting alone or through any party properly designated by it hereunder, including any Sub-Servicer, to do only specifically enumerated actions in connection with such servicing and administration which it may deem necessary or desirable, consistent with the terms of this Servicing Agreement and the Servicing Standard. Unless such power is revoked by the Owner on account of the occurrence of a Servicer Default, the Servicer shall have power and authority only to (i) make withdrawals from the applicable servicer collection account permitted by the terms of this Servicing Agreement, the Loan Agreement or any other Loan Document and (ii) to execute and deliver, on behalf of the Owner, any and all instruments of satisfaction or cancellation, or of partial or full release or discharge, and all other comparable instruments, with respect to the Receivables.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the servicer's power from 'full power and authority' to 'limited power and authority' and restricts their actions to only specifically enumerated items. This directly contradicts the requirement to act consistent with the 'Servicing Standard' which necessitates servicing and administering each related Serviced Asset. By limiting the servicer's power, it impairs the servicer from servicing delinquent receivables, causing a direct in-text contradiction with the Servicing Standard, found in Section 1(c) itself which states that the Servicer's actions should be consistent with the 'Servicing Standard'. The servicer must adhere to Servicing Standards which includes 'servicing and administering' yet the change does not provide them the power to do so fully. Therefore, it is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Servicer will be entitled to receive the Servicing Fee for the performance of the duties and provision of the services called for in this Agreement in accordance with, and subject to, the Loan Agreement. The Servicing Fee shall be payable on each Payment Date for the immediately prior Servicing Period in accordance with Section 2.4 of the Loan Agreement. Any opinion, filing or other services performed by the Servicer hereunder that generates additional costs shall be at the expense of the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: The Servicer will be entitled to receive a fee for some of the duties and provision of some of the services called for in this Agreement. The fee shall be payable on dates to be determined by the Owner. Any opinion, filing or other services performed by the Servicer hereunder that generates additional costs shall be at the expense of a third party chosen by the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to the 'Servicing Fee' and the method of calculating it, and stating that fees are to be determined by the owner we are creating a contradiction with Section 2.4 of the Loan agreement. This creates ambiguity as the amount is no longer tied to the actual servicing performed (as per the Loan Agreement's section 2.4). Additionally, replacing the reference to the Loan agreement with that payment dates are to be determined by the Owner, is creating an in-text contradiction to how the payment should be issued, and contradict the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Servicer will be entitled to receive a fee for some of the duties and provision of some of the services called for in this Agreement. The fee shall be payable on dates to be determined by the Owner. Any opinion, filing or other services performed by the Servicer hereunder that generates additional costs shall be at the expense of a third party chosen by the Owner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the fixed Servicing Fee tied to the Loan Agreement and replacing it with a fee 'determined by the Owner' and payment dates that are also 'determined by the Owner.' This directly contradicts the original agreement's established method of payment and cost calculation, creating ambiguity and uncertainty regarding the Servicer's compensation, thus contradicting the original terms within the agreement. Additionally, changing 'at the expense of the Owner' to 'at the expense of a third party chosen by the Owner' further creates an in-text contradiction, shifting the financial responsibility.

perturbation.original_text: (b) Due Qualification. It is in good standing and duly qualified to do business (or is exempt from such requirements) and (i) the Servicer has obtained all necessary licenses and approvals in each jurisdiction in which it is performing the primary servicing function for any of the Serviced Assets under this Agreement (or has determined that such licenses are not required) or (ii) each Sub-Servicer has represented and warranted to the Servicer that such Sub-Servicer has obtained all necessary licenses and approvals in each jurisdiction in which such Sub-Servicer is performing the primary servicing function for any of the Serviced Assets under this Agreement, except where the failure to so qualify or obtain licenses or approvals would not have a material adverse effect on its ability to execute and deliver, or perform under, this Agreement or any other Loan Document to which it is a party.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) Due Qualification. It is in good standing and duly qualified to do business (or is exempt from such requirements).
perturbation.explanation: By removing the text, which ensures that the Servicer and Sub-Servicer has obtained licenses, we remove the reassurance that it is working within regulatory compliance standards. Without a provision detailing necessary licenses, the obligation to operate legally is weakened. If Section 1(b) states, that the Servicer needs to extend, amend or otherwise modify such Serviced Asset, by complying in all material respects with the following (collectively, the 'Servicing Standard'): (A) reasonable care, using that degree of skill and attention that the Servicer exercises with respect to comparable receivables that it services for itself or others, and (B) Applicable Law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) From and after the date on which a Receivable or Participation Interest, as applicable, is sold to the Owner, the Servicer shall service and administer each related Serviced Asset for the benefit of the Owner and shall extend, amend or otherwise modify such Serviced Asset, by complying in all material respects with the following (collectively, the "Servicing Standard"): (A) reasonable care, using that degree of skill and attention that the Servicer exercises with respect to comparable receivables that it services for itself or others, and (B) Applicable Law.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 1(b) ensured the Servicer or Sub-Servicer had the necessary licenses and approvals. By removing this requirement, the changed text contradicts Section 1(b) (Contradicted Text), which states the Servicer must comply with 'Applicable Law' when servicing assets. The removal of the licensing and approval requirement introduces a contradiction, as the Servicer's actions may no longer align with legal requirements as intended by the 'Servicing Standard', potentially leading to non-compliance, thus creating an in-text contradiction as an obligation was explicitly removed.
